response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a capable polyglot engineer with a long track record across web, mobile, and systems domains, but the resume lacks the core technical foundation this role requires. Java appears in the skills list but is barely evidenced in actual project work, Kotlin is absent entirely, and there is no mention of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM-centric enterprise framework. The one relevant data point — working on NI LabWindows/CVI at National Instruments — was a C/C++ IDE, not a JVM tooling context. The candidate's primary professional identity is TypeScript/Node.js, which does not transfer to the deep JVM internals and enterprise framework expertise this position demands.
strengths: Demonstrated interest in IDE internals through work on NI LabWindows/CVI, a C IDE for engineers
Developer tooling mindset evidenced by building test runners, assertion libraries, and MCP frameworks
Long tenure across complex, large-scale distributed systems with high-performance and reliability constraints
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin production experience despite listing Java as a skill; Kotlin is entirely absent
Zero exposure to Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework in any listed role
Core professional stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang — fundamentally misaligned with a JVM-focused IDE engineering role
